block_01: Festivities, schedules, and hints for promotion in Latin America and Europe
block_03: How does your audience celebrate the winter holidays — and does it, really?
block_04: Today, we will review the Christmas and New Year traditions of our latest top-performing countries of Latin America and Europe in terms of FTD. Use these insights to fuel your promo with extra chills and motivation!
block_06: Winter holidays are loud in Latin America. The celebrations begin in December with the birth of baby Jesus and end in January with the Feast of the Adoration of the Magi.
block_07: Christmas is rather family-oriented, while the New Year celebrations are more party-ish: people go to the beach, dance, and watch fireworks.
block_08: Hint: New Year’s Eve is the time for all sorts of money-related rituals. Play them out in your promo! For example, it is customary to wear yellow underwear on New Year's Eve — this should bring success in financial affairs for the year to come. Pomegranate seeds and bay leaf put in the wallet are also believed to bring luck with money.
block_11: Europe is a keeper of the age-old Christmas and New Year traditions. Santa Claus, Christmas tree, markets, bells — many holiday mascots originated there and later spreaded all over the Christian world. Despite the fact that holiday traditions in most EU countries are similar, each one has its own national flavor.
block_12: Hint: In Europe, Christmas Eve is a bigger deal than Christmas Day. Schedule your promo activities before December 24 — this is when the public interest spikes.
block_13: Also, any European wishes for what’s called “White Christmas” — simply put, a snowy holiday (that doesn’t happen all that often). Add those snowflakes to your visuals to cheer up your European audience.
block_14: Next time, we will share the holiday insights for Asia and Africa. Stay posted!
